meaning: adj.缺乏才智的,愚钝的,没有智能力的
examples: He was not unintelligent, but he was lazy.
Being dyslexic does not mean that one is unintelligent. Far from it.
Teenagers often struggle with an overwhelming sense that nobody likes them, that they're not as good as other people, that they are failures, losers, ugly or unintelligent.
There was no suggestion that the jury in that case was unintelligent, far from it, it was suggested that the 13-month trial was so fiendishly complex that even Mensa members would have struggled.
Those zombie-like souls are not necessarily the unintelligent.
